results.rendered_html: śūrpa—ṇakhā (rarely -ṇakhī) f. (wrongly -nakhā, ˚khī; cf.  Pāṇ. iv, 1, 58 ) ‘having fingernails like w˚ fans’, N. of the sister of Rāvaṇa (she fell in love with Rāma-candra and, being rejected by him and insulted by Sītā's laughter, assumed a hideous form and threatened to eat her up, but was beaten off by Lakṣmaṇa, who cut off her ears and nose and thus doubly disfigured her; in revenge she incited her brother to carry off Sītā),  MBh. ;  R.

results.rendered_html: śūrpaṇakhā (śūrpa + nakha) f. N. pr. Schol. zu  P. 4,1,58 . 8,4,3 . einer Rākṣasī, einer Schwester Rāvaṇaʼs,  ŚABDAM.  und  ŚABDAR. _im_ ŚKDR.   MBH. 3,15896 . 15900 .  R. 1,1,44. fg.  (48 GORR. ). 3,23,12 . 6,108,35 . 7,9,35 .  RAGH. 12,38 .  Verz. d. B. H. No. 536 . ˚ṇakhī (gegen  P. 4,1,58 )  ŚABDAM.  und  ŚABDAR. _im_ ŚKDR.   R. 6,82,103. fg.   BHĀG. P. 9,10,4 . Hier und da fälschlich śūrpanakhā geschrieben.
